blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: What about after? When you're done with school.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: You should. You're already the kind of person who figures things out.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Most people wait until something tells them who they are. You're not waiting. You went and tried out. You wrote things down in class. You sat on the steps.
She said the steps like it was a small brave thing. Maybe it was.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: What about you? After senior year.
That was the question Jonathan told me to ask. Something I actually wanted to know.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: That's the first time you've asked me something.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I don't know yet. My parents want one thing. I want something close to it but not exactly it.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: What's the difference between what they want and what you want?
She smiled when she said it. Like it was a thought she'd had a hundred times and had finally found the words for it.
The bus came. The 34. She didn't move right away.
